entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 15 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-15 04:32AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Australia news LIVE: Fair Work Commission raises minimum wage by 5.2 per cent; Greens call for energy market change; RBA predicts 7 per cent inflation by end of 2022
  • From the Archives, 1982: Scrape and paint rejuvenates Mr Squiggle
  • Axed Huawei Metronet contract to cost WA taxpayers $6.6 million
  • Smith ‘dropped the ball’ but secret data clears Bulldogs of major drug problem
  • A Squid Game reality show? Did Netflix miss the point of its biggest ever release?
  • Gawn to miss key stretch; new Blue given huge task; Daniel re-signs with Dogs
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • Aussie horse Nature Strip wins at Ascot
  • ‘Tip over the edge’: 5.2% wage rise hard to absorb, employers argue
  • The easiest ways to save money at the supermarket
  • Pop star Lizzo owned her mistake - and won my respect
  • Don’t let winter stop you from exercising. Here’s how to tackle it
  • NRL announces NRLW expansion
  • Woman holidays with ex: 'When you break up, but the trip was nonrefundable'
  • Council eyeing underused commercial sites to help housing squeeze
  • Blackouts dodged as regulator demands answers from power companies

  • Trump skewered by closest advisors during Capitol riot hearing

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

  • In US-Saudi Reset, the Kingdom Holds All the Cards
  • Saudi Megaproject Is Big on Hubris and Low on Practicality
  • The Yen Won’t Be Moved by ‘90s Nostalgia
  • What Are Carbon Offsets and How Many Really Work?
  • The Boom in Private Markets Has Transformed Finance. Here’s How
  • Why Closer Ties Between Russia and China Have Democracies Worried
  • The War in Ukraine Is Speeding Europe’s Pivot to Renewables
  • The good news about gun control isn’t the bipartisan deal
  • I thought the Jan. 6 committee wouldn’t matter. I was wrong.
  • The U.S. must oppose India’s rising Islamophobia
  • Your questions about covid-19, answered by Dr. Leana Wen
  • A wave of violence as the court prepares to rule
  • In Ukraine, is the balance tipping in Moscow’s favor? Not yet.
  • It’s almost as if Democrats are trying to make sure Trump wins in 2024
  • Is AI sentient? Wrong question.
  • Greed is dead. Long live greed!
  • Why prosecutorial reform will outlive Chesa Boudin’s recall
  • Michael R. Jackson’s ‘big, Black and queer’ musical, ‘A Strange Loop’
  • Pennsylvania’s vote-counting rules risk 2024 electoral chaos

  • How aggressively should liberals attack the Supreme Court?
  • Americans have gone back to work. Now we can move toward steady growth.
  • Out of bounds
  • How Ukraine’s ‘women warriors’ are playing a vital role in the war
  • Has Justin Trudeau been ‘Americanized’ — or is he just left-wing?
  • I saw Mugabe wreck a democracy. The Jan. 6 hearings matter — a lot.
  • Fairfax County is a leader in promoting red-flag laws. Others should follow.
  • Is Virginia’s lieutenant governor paying attention to her friends?
  • Reparations should include funding for high-quality early education
  • A rise in tensions as the Supreme Court nears the end of its term
  • Mobile voting is not the answer to low turnout and disenfranchisement
  • Mental health care is adversely affected by focus on the bottom line
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 15 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-15 04:04AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong legislators question transport bureau’s proposal for congestion levy to ease traffic jams at cross-harbour tunnels
  • Hong Kong slips one place to fourth most expensive city for luxury spending as hotel rates take a huge hit from pandemic, Julius Baer report finds
  • Exclusive interview: Hong Kong’s first chief justice Andrew Li on whether all judges in city should be Chinese nationals ahead of 2047
  • Climate Change: China’s firms lag behind Asia, North America, Europe in net-zero targets with only 4 per cent adherence, study finds
  • Historic Hong Kong mansion handed over to pro-Beijing think tank to transform into healthy living centre
  • February 8, 2016: Night of mob violence in Mong Kok leaves Hong Kong on edge
  • Beijing reveals blueprint for Nansha, with incentives that could draw young Hongkongers into calling the Guangzhou district home
  • Zuckerberg’s Meta to push metaverse initiatives to Hong Kong cafes and schools as interest in virtual world hots up
  • November 1, 2014: Expat banker Rurik Jutting arrested over murder of 2 women
  • HKMA sells US$1.74 billion to defend Hong Kong currency peg from capital outflow ahead of expected US interest rate rise
  • October 1, 2012: Deadly ferry crash off Hong Kong’s Lamma Island kills 39
  • Cathay Pacific expecting better showing in first half of 2022 despite staying in the red, after strong cargo performance and cost-cutting moves
  • Hong Kong health officials change how they present daily number of Covid-19 infections
  • August 23, 2010: 7 Hong Kong tourists and guide killed in Manila bus hostage crisis
  • Hong Kong customs seizes phones, pharmaceuticals in haul worth HK$7.5 million hidden in excavators
  • July 1, 2003: 500,000 take to Hong Kong’s streets in protest against proposed national security legislation
  • Consumption vouchers: retailers, e-payment service providers offer promotions worth billions to entice Hongkongers ahead of roll-out
  • June 23, 2003: Hong Kong free of Sars, WHO declares
  • June 26, 1999: Beijing approves Hong Kong Basic Law reinterpretation on right of abode
  • China’s economic slowdown moderates in May: industrial production picks up as retail sales decline
  • China refrains from policy rate cut, defying market expectations once again
  • How China’s belt and road diplomacy could end the Ukraine conflict for good
  • Veteran Chinese human rights lawyers expected to face trial soon
  • Chubb CEO Evan Greenberg urges US businesses to stay engaged in China market
  • Backlash against Russians living abroad is all too familiar for anxious Chinese expats
  • For China’s elite graduates, career dreams take a back seat to stability in coronavirus-hit job market
  • US drug supply chain alternative to China might run through Canada, Latin America
  • Chinese students’ dream device defeats Japan’s most powerful supercomputer in world contest
  • Dozens of countries call out China at UN over Xinjiang abuse allegations
  • Where did water on the moon come from? Chinese scientists find clues in lunar samples
  • Apple CEO Tim Cook expects continued growth for ‘cutting-edge’ Chinese software developers amid rising geopolitical tensions
  • China’s consumption problem rooted in fears and uncertainties, while vouchers have limited appeal
  • Chinese concerns rise over US policy shift on Taiwan
  • China still backing Poland gateway plan despite impact of Ukraine war
  • Career diplomat Le Yucheng moves to Chinese state broadcasting body as deputy chief
  • Coronavirus: Taiwan’s economic outlook dims as Omicron outbreak casts a shadow over growth
  • Chinese AI giant Megvii’s chief scientist Sun Jian dies at age 45 of sudden illness
  • China says it has released Bloomberg staffer Haze Fan on bail
  • Omicron sub-variants latest challenge for China’s zero-Covid policy
  • China calls for overseas arrivals to be monitored for signs of monkeypox
  • Chinese smartphone brand Meizu sold to carmaker Geely as smart vehicles become latest frontier for Big Tech
  • Fears of data abuse as Chinese health code turns red for financial scandal protesters
  • Shanghai merchants, hard-hit by Covid-19 lockdown, delay reopening of Qipu Road clothing market, demand rent relief
  • Australia-China relations: Canberra’s foreign minister Penny Wong to travel to Solomon Islands amid China security pact concerns
  • Deaf Indian boy trapped in well with snakes and scorpions is rescued after four days
  • Coronavirus: New Zealand to eliminate pre-departure Covid-19 tests for incoming passengers
  • India all but sinks WTO sustainable fishing deal; China among contributors to global overfishing
  • Can Sri Lanka tourism recover from ‘triple whammy’ of terrorism, Covid-19 and economic crisis?
  • BTS announce temporary break, with plans for solo releases before reuniting as group – though label denies it’s a ‘hiatus’
  • Are India’s billion-dollar trade ties with Gulf states in ‘peril’ amid Prophet Mohammed row?
  • Thai cat with Covid suspected to have infected human
  • Malaysia eases chicken export ban but not for Singapore’s favourite ‘broiler’
  • Take holiday to grow your own food, civil servants told by Sri Lankan government
  • AirAsia turns to India to fill gap left by absent China tourists due to Covid-19 travel curbs
  • Iko Uwais, Indonesian martial arts star who rose to fame in Hollywood and Netflix’s Wu Assassins accused of assaulting interior designer
  • India’s women, Muslims targeted by nationalist YouTube influencers, report finds
  • Cambodia jails ‘Lady Liberty’ lawyer for treason over Sam Rainsy’s failed return from exile, alongside dozens of others
  • Australia-China relations: Albanese says Beijing must lift sanctions on exports to reset ties
  • In Ukraine-Russia war, echoes of WWI with artillery duels, trenches and shell-blasted landscapes
  • Netflix plans Squid Game reality show with US$4.5 million prize, no fatal consequences
  • Canada and Denmark settle decades-old ‘whiskey war’ over Arctic island
  • UK cancels first flight to deport asylum seekers to Rwanda after European court ruling
  • Iran preparing for rocket launch, satellite images suggest amid nuclear tensions
  • Jailed Putin critic Alexei Navalny moved to high-security penal colony
  • Happy the elephant is not a person, top New York court rules – she is a ‘magnificent creature’
  • EU turns to Israel for gas after ‘blackmail’ by Russia over Ukraine war support
  • Pope says Russia’s ‘ferocious’ invasion perhaps provoked and we don’t see the ‘whole drama’
  • Ukraine war: ‘Surrender or die’ choice in Sievierodonetsk after Russian forces destroy bridges to city
  • Gruesome scenes and anguish in Ukraine frontline city Lysychansk: ‘They bomb and bomb’
  • ‘War good for business’ as drive to arm Ukraine looms over French expo
  • Ukraine exhumes bodies with ‘knees shot, hands tied’ from makeshift forest graves
  • US likely to single out Tesla for crashes involving Autopilot after collecting data on automated driving
  • China releases new national climate change strategy to build resilience against global warming by 2035
  • China to test market appetite for debt with biggest offshore bond sale since 2016
  • In Hong Kong’s property market, HK$10 million no longer buys what it did in 1997
  • Developers’ aggressive loan schemes could put financially weak buyers at risk if Hong Kong home prices fall, analysts say
  • Hong Kong malls, retailers tap into AI analytics to get smarter after losing US$16 billion of sales in post-2018 industry slump
  • China’s 618 online shopping festival more muted than previous years as platforms shift focus from sales to service quality
  • As the industrial metaverse grows, the platform must remain open and available
  • Yuan currency most exposed to climate change – could lose half its value in 50 years, Barclays says
  • US senator questions whether HSBC was pressured to suspend banker over controversial climate-change comments
  • GoGoX defies IPO drought as it pushes through downsized Hong Kong offering targeting US$85.5 million, down from US$500 million
  • Google debate over sentient AI overshadows more pressing issues like prejudice and exploitation, researchers say
  • SoftBank plans additional London listing for Arm initial public offering
  • ByteDance’s Hong Kong entity registers multiple mainland subsidiaries in move seen as prelude to possible listing in the city
  • The best cart noodles in Hong Kong according to a chef, where he goes for spare ribs rice, and memorable meals with his boss, ‘Demon Chef’ Alvin Leung
  • Airport chaos as surge in flight bookings catches travel trade on the hop, with long queues and cancellations
  • What is amla? The Indian gooseberry hailed as a superfood for its apparent anticancer, anti-diabetes and anti-ageing properties
  • The Black Phone movie review: Ethan Hawke plays a sadistic child murderer in Doctor Strange director’s effective horror thriller
  • Are bleached eyebrows right for you? Expert tips on a viral ’90s beauty trend made popular by Kendall Jenner, Maisie Williams and Lizzo
  • Netflix K-drama review: Our Blues – Lee Byung-hun and Kim Hye-ja provide heart-rending ending for ensemble drama
  • Blood donation is easy and painless, says record donor in Hong Kong, who’s 70 and has given blood 190 times. How and why you should too
  • Japanese artist who was an ‘Instagram star’ years before the internet was born shows works in Hong Kong
  • The 15 best new Hollywood movies in cinemas this summer, from Elvis to Minions: The Rise of Gru and Thor: Love and Thunder
  • Man who knows where to find the best pizza in Hong Kong on his rating system, pies that surprised, and plans to meet his followers
  • 6 K-pop groups who stayed together after leaving their agencies: Got7, 2PM, Girls’ Generation, Mamamoo, Apink and Highlight all made comebacks with their OG members
  • Meet Kim Se-jeong, Today’s Webtoon star: the K-drama actress debuted as a K-pop idol in IOI before acting in K-dramas like Business Proposal on Netflix – but why is she called ‘Korea’s Emma Stone’?
  • Zhou Rui’s provocative fashion: she’s dressed Blackpink’s Lisa, Madison Beer and other stars within just three years of launching her brand thanks to her style that ‘isn’t limited by gender or size’
  • Meet Riley Keough, Elvis Presley’s granddaughter – she modelled for Dior before acting and directing, and ‘loved’ stepdad Michael Jackson, but why didn’t she act in the Elvis biopic?
  • Who is Rachel Zegler, the new lead in Hunger Games’ prequel? She got her break in Steven Spielberg’s West Side Story, walks red carpets in Dior, and covers Lady Gaga on YouTube
  • Savoury desserts? Fine dining chefs are giving sweet treats a new take, from Michelin-starred Noma in Denmark’s dish with oyster stock, to Sawaan in Thailand’s crab claw-inspired concept
  • 5 K-drama stars with odd jobs before the fame: Kim Tae-ri worked in a supermarket while Kim Min-seok was a cook in a Japanese restaurant … what about So Ji-sub, Go Min-si and Pyo Ye-jin?
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Coinbase to cut almost a fifth of staff as crypto crunch worsens
  • What were all those 6,200 Coinbasers doing anyway?
  • Celsius bid to rival Wall St with crypto lending scuppered by risky bets

  • Is ‘Made in Japan’ back with the weaker yen?

  • Apple faces German antitrust probe over app tracking rules

  • The peculiar economics of autonomous cabs
  • Crypto exchanges slash jobs as market turmoil triggers trading downturn

  • JGBs/BlueBay: inflation may help short sellers slay the widow maker

entry.title: Guardian: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Lightyear’s anti-gay critics can buzz off, says Chris Evans

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Tokyo dentist bakes bread you can really sink your teeth into

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 14 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Jan. 6 Panel Tracks How Trump Created and Spread Election Lies
